items.title: USDA: SNAP max benefit is $298/month for a 1-person household (Oct 2025–Sept 2026)
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service lists the maximum SNAP monthly allotment for the 48 contiguous states and D.C. as $298 for a 1-person household and $994 for a 4-person household. The agency notes these figures apply for applications between Oct. 1, 2025 and Sept. 30, 2026 (amounts vary in Alaska, Hawaii, Guam, and the U.S. Virgin Islands).

items.title: IRS: 2026 EITC max credit rises to $8,231 for families with 3+ qualifying children
items.summary: The IRS says the maximum Earned Income Tax Credit for tax year 2026 is $8,231 for qualifying taxpayers with three or more qualifying children (up from $8,046 for tax year 2025). The IRS notes these 2026 inflation adjustments generally apply to tax returns filed in 2027.

items.title: IRS: 2026 adoption credit increases to $17,670 (with $5,120 potentially refundable)
items.summary: The IRS’ 2026 inflation adjustments set the maximum adoption credit at up to $17,670 of qualified adoption expenses (up from $17,280 for 2025). The IRS also lists $5,120 as the amount of the credit that may be refundable.

items.title: IRS: Employer-provided childcare credit max increases to $500,000 for 2026
items.summary: In its 2026 inflation adjustments release, the IRS says the maximum employer-provided childcare tax credit increases from $150,000 to $500,000 for tax year 2026 ($600,000 if the employer is an eligible small business). This change is part of the broader set of inflation adjustments the IRS publishes for 2026.

items.title: SSA: Social Security and SSI benefits increase 2.8% in 2026
items.summary: SSA announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026. SSA says nearly 71 million Social Security beneficiaries will see the increase beginning in January 2026, and increased payments to nearly 7.5 million SSI recipients begin December 31, 2025.

items.title: SSA: 2026 SSI federal payment standard is $994/month (individual) and $1,491/month (couple)
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 COLA fact sheet lists the SSI federal payment standard at $994 per month for an eligible individual and $1,491 per month for an eligible couple. The same fact sheet includes estimated average benefits after the COLA, including $1,630 for all disabled workers.

items.title: SSA: Maximum earnings subject to Social Security tax increases to $184,500 in 2026
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 COLA announcement says the taxable maximum (the maximum amount of earnings subject to the Social Security tax) is slated to rise to $184,500, up from $176,100. SSA includes the same $184,500 figure in its 2026 COLA fact sheet.

items.title: KFF: Medicaid/CHIP enrollment was 77.7 million as of June 2025 (down 18% from March 2023)
items.summary: KFF reports total Medicaid and CHIP enrollment was 77.7 million as of June 2025, reflecting an 18% decline from March 2023 as states continued the post-pandemic eligibility redetermination process. KFF also notes enrollment remained 9% higher than pre-pandemic levels in February 2020.

items.title: KFF: H.R. 1 Medicaid provisions estimated to reduce federal Medicaid spending by $911B (14%) over 10 years
items.summary: KFF’s Medicaid budget survey overview says Medicaid provisions in H.R. 1 are estimated to reduce federal Medicaid spending by $911 billion (14%) over a decade. KFF highlights specific components including provider tax policy changes estimated at $191 billion over ten years and revisions to State Directed Payments estimated at $149 billion.

items.title: FSA: Apply by April 23, 2026 for Title III designation and a waiver of the FWS/FSEOG non-federal share
items.summary: Federal Student Aid published an electronic announcement reminding institutions to apply by April 23, 2026 for designation as a Title III institution. The announcement also notes eligible institutions can receive a waiver of the non-federal share requirement for Federal Work-Study (FWS) and Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grant (FSEOG).
